author: [HEIM] SDU pimpUstur
content: Realistically, will we see C4 and Zink launch this year ? I need to know, so I can assess if I want to keep paying for this Zink subscription. It already feels abit misleading considering it was advertised as launching in March, but is now "to be determined". Every month that passes without Zink launching hurts every single person who is invested into the subscription and trying to farm zXP, is this being taken into consideration ? A little bit more clarity on what is going on overall on C4 and Zink front would be great.

author: Chet Roberts [ROME]
content:
@SW4GN3R  Serious question from someone rooting for the project.
 Does ATMTA currently have regulatory clarity around ATLAS and POLIS, or is it operating under the same uncertainty most crypto projects are dealing with right now?
 While the company is still relatively small it may be somewhat off the regulatory radar, but if Star Atlas succeeds and grows into what many of us believe it can become, that will obviously change.
 I’m curious how the team is thinking about that future and preparing for it.

author: Chet Roberts [ROME]
content: I'm a card caring member of the XRP army and I seen what they tried to do to Ripple is the only reason I'm asking. I have had to deal with bureaucrats irl and the last thing we want is to be blind sided. I'm not suggesting you have not thought about it only that it's something that can't be ignored. As an entrepreneur I understand risk taking but it was just something I wanted to check off in my due diligence box before I go to the next level in S.A. I did some speculating in the early crypto days using a vpn to trade on foreign exchanges when I need to but now having lived through the dot com bubble I'm a conservative utility kinda guy who buys pretend space ships.

content: Q&A with the team in here please

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Hi team! Is it really that complicated to create a diamond crew pack? It's not very reassuring... hang in there!

reply.preview: Hi team! Is it really that complicated to create a diamond crew pack? It's not very reassuring... hang in there!

author: ONIZUK4 INKICHI [ROME]
content: Yeah, there are unfortunately some complexities. Because we elected to use the cNFT standard, there are pre-configured parameters around the merkle tree regarding the crew mint. We need to modify those parameters to mint a "new type" with different probabilities. Plus a little bit of art. It's relatively not that difficult for us to do, but given the limited resources, it's difficult for us to find time to get it done. We're working on it though.

reply.preview: Yeah, there are unfortunately some complexities. Because we elected to use the cNFT standard, there are pre-configured parameters around the merkle tree regarding the crew mint. We need to modify those parameters to mint a "new type" with different probabilities. Plus a little bit of art. It's relatively not that difficult for us to do, but given the limited resources, it's difficult for us to find time to get it done. We're working on it though.

author: Xcode [STAR]
content:
Just to piggy back on something @Jindo | Star Atlas Mod mentioned in FR-Chat. We are building an in app purchases page for Holosim right now, where you'll be able to purchse a limited number of crew. I expected that to be ready in a few weeks, and would make a nice catch up mechanic. 

 In app purchases would be entirely done through stripe (credit card) payments. 

Items purchased in Holosim will stay in Holosim or be consumable. 

That being said, Holosim will continue to award mainnet items as end of chapter awards, to encourage  new users to explor the Mainnet / Zink products.

Also worth mentioning, I'm planning on capping the total number of crew you can get through the daily check in. (If you start the chapter late you can still reach the same maximum , and if you purchase the battle pass late you wouldn't lose out on those either). Other ways to get crew outside the storyline would be some new social quests I'm working on and the in app purchases options.

That being said, Holosim will continue to award mainnet items as end of chapter awards, to encourage  new users to explor the Mainnet / Zink products.  Also worth mentioning, I'm planning on capping the total number of crew you can get through the daily check in. (If you start the chapter late you can still reach the same maximum , and if you purchase the battle pass late you wouldn't lose out on those either). Other ways to get crew outside the storyline would be some new social quests I'm working on and the in app purchases options.

reply.preview: Just to piggy back on something @Jindo | Star Atlas Mod mentioned in FR-Chat. We are building an in app purchases page for Holosim right now, where you'll be able to purchse a limited number of crew. I expected that to be ready in a few weeks, and would make a nice catch up mechanic. In app purchases would be entirely done through stripe (credit card) payments. Items purchased in Holosim will stay in Holosim or be consumable. That being said, Holosim will continue to award mainnet items as end of chapter awards, to encourage new users to explor the Mainnet / Zink products. Also worth mentioning, I'm planning on capping the total number of crew you can get through the daily check in. (If you start the chapter late you can still reach the same maximum , and if you purchase the battle pass late you wouldn't lose out on those either). Other ways to get crew outside the storyline would be some new social quests I'm working on and the in app purchases options.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Thanks for checking. I'll double check with Swift, but I don't think we've pushed any updates recently.  If the graph is strictly counting requests, it may have been a cascading type scenario. Transactions fail which triggers retries, which results in more transactions over a shorter period of time.  I think you guys are able to see what types of transactions come through. If you're seeing a bunch of non-SAGE transactions, then yeah we should probably rotate.

author: [AEP] Lerinor [STAR]
content: My biggest gripe with Holosim combat is the engagement system. There's no meaningful warning and no significant mechanics to defend your territory.  As it stands today, if the aggressor decides to attack, you have maybe 60 seconds to react. And with a coordinated attack, it's simply not possible to mount a coordinated defense in that time window.  I strongly believe that this type of game would benefit from a more forgiving combat engagement system. One that provides more notice to the defenders and gives them the opportunity to try to mount a defense.  My question to the team is - are you open to re-engineering the engagement system, or is the style we see in Holosim the intended design?  I'm not trying to open a debate, but rather trying to determine what type of game to expect with C4. If the decisions have already been made, I can make an informed decision on if that is the type of game that I would find enjoyable.  Thanks for all your hard work, and thanks for your consideration.

author: [AEP] Mendizo
content: It’s not final. PTR is the perfect time to provide feedback like this. We didn’t get to a place with combat for PTR that was worth putting out the new system so we have the fallback of a system closer to holosim but it’s not intended to be the final system so as we finish that system for actual launch during PTR then we’ll of course try to incorporate new feedback.

author: bunthius [STAR]
content: Maybe I'm mixing up mechanics, especially for future state vs PTR (which sounds like more interim state)... but a big part of the mechanic will be that only certain "lanes" allow attacks, based on what regions are already controlled, right?  So, to the spirit of Groove's earlier point, if it was completely open-ended in terms of potential attacks, like it is in Holosim right now, that would be extremely tough. The upper hand is always with the attacker. But, if we indeed have these "lanes" (which does allow flanking in certain fashion IIRC), and you know the path that attackers must take, then it is easier to defend. It is up to each side to mount an effective defense, and it's a tradeoff of resources/assets for defense vs. for production.  Is that roughly on track with the plans?
